what_it_does: Documents the deployment, configuration, benchmark results, operational quirks, and unresolved measurement gaps for the cyankiwi/Qwen3.5-4B-AWQ-4bit model running under vLLM as the default chat service.
observations: Frontmatter marks the model as status deployed-default, runtime vllm, port 8000, added 2026-05-26, last characterised 2026-05-27.
The model is an AWQ Int4 vision-language build with hybrid Mamba-attention architecture and a native context of 262,144 tokens.
Production context is capped at 4K; long-context testing reached about 30K; 32K-262K band is unmeasured.
A patched chat template disables thinking by default because the stock template emits thinking tokens that break tool calling.
Tool parser is qwen3_xml; structured output and tool calling both passed 3/3 checks in the file.
Concurrency sweep shows a throughput sweet spot at 32 concurrent requests, with TTFT degrading sharply past 64.
Prefix caching is enabled and produced 40-110ms TTFT across 8-turn multi-turn tests.
Vision capability is present but explicitly noted as untested for OCR, document understanding, and image-grounded Q&A.
The model can co-reside with bge-m3 and jina-embeddings-v4 at roughly 15.5 GiB total VRAM; a fourth large model would OOM.
Longest measured sustained run was only 8 minutes; multi-day stability is listed as open.
standouts: The no-think chat-template patch is required for reliable tool calling — a concrete operational workaround.
Throughput peaks at concurrency 32 but stays flat while latency explodes past 128, making queue behavior a hard limit.
Vision tower consumes VRAM even for text-only requests, and disabling it is not supported in vLLM 0.21.
Hybrid Mamba-attention prefix caching is flagged as experimental in logs, though no regressions were observed.
Co-residency with two embedding services has been verified, giving a precise VRAM ceiling.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with type, id, filename, status, runtime, port, dates, and tags.
Follows a hub-and-spoke wiki-link pattern with a models index and related operations files.
Presents facts in tables for at-a-glance metadata, concurrency sweeps, long-input prefill, and long-output decode.
Separates source rationale, tested performance, known-good launch config, quirks, open items, and connects-to sections.
Cites an external benchmark factsheet in ~/vllm-tests while keeping a canonical synthesis in the file.
Breaks convention by using future dates (2026) and hardcoded home-directory paths for scripts and templates.
concerns: Native context is 262K but only 30K has been tested, leaving a large operational gap.
Vision is listed as a fit for several needs despite never being evaluated on this server.
Longest observed run is 8 minutes, yet the model is deployed-default for production chat.
The claim that 0.8B is too small for production quality is asserted without supporting evidence in the file.
Patched template and launch script paths are tied to a specific home directory, reducing portability.
The file mentions vLLM 0.21 in quirks but does not state the exact serving version in the frontmatter.
notes: A heavily benchmarked operational model card for the default chat model, with explicit gaps around vision, 32K-262K context, and long-run stability.
questions: Who or what is 'Tim' referenced in the open vision tasks?
What GPU hardware and driver/CUDA versions were used for the benchmarks?
What exact vLLM version is running in production?
How was 'production quality' defined when rejecting the 0.8B variant?
What does 'mode set to align' mean for the Mamba cache, and what are the failure modes?
What is the model license and upstream data cutoff?
How does quality compare between this AWQ Int4 build and the upstream non-quantized model?
purpose_vs_actual: matches — it presents itself as a model hub entry and delivers deployment facts, performance tables, launch config, and open questions.
apparent_themes: Evidence-based model selection and capacity planning
Reliable tool calling and structured output
intention_signals: To make the default chat model reproducible and operable by centralizing its launch config, measured behavior, known quirks, and remaining unknowns in one linked ledger entry.
novelty: It pairs a quantized small-model deployment card with extensive vLLM concurrency and long-context benchmarks, and explicitly documents a patched chat-template workaround needed for reliable tool calling.
contribution: A file-local, evidence-backed record of how this quantized model is served, including launch flags, performance sweet spots, co-residency constraints, and a catalog of untested capabilities — useful for operational decisions and future regression checks.
summary_for_embedding: A model card for the deployed-default cyankiwi/Qwen3.5-4B-AWQ-4bit vision-language chat model, covering vLLM launch configuration, throughput/latency benchmarks, tool-calling setup, a no-think chat-template patch, co-residency limits, and open measurement gaps.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the operational source of truth for running and reasoning about the default quantized chat model on this substrate.
